theory.intro: If you cut a shape into pieces and rearrange them into a different shape, the total area stays the same. This idea turns hard shapes into easy ones.
facts: Cutting and rearranging **preserves area** — no surface is added or lost.
A parallelogram can be cut and rearranged into a **rectangle** with the same base and height.
Two equal triangles can be joined to make a parallelogram or rectangle.
steps: Cut the shape into pieces (often along a straight line).
Move the pieces to form a simpler shape, such as a rectangle.
The area is unchanged, so find the area of the simpler shape.
